name: Venezuela: INHRR registration process and special-import authorization
access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (regulatory pathway). The INHRR Review Board's 'special authorization for non-registered pharmaceutical products' is the key exception mechanism that legally permits importation of unregistered drugs, valid for a maximum of 6 months per authorization. This is the primary legal route for accessing innovative oncology and rare-disease drugs that have not completed full Venezuelan sanitary registration.
procurement_channels: Once INHRR special authorization is granted, the MPPS issues an importation permit. Channels include: licensed importers/distributors (Drolanca, Drogueria Los Andes), hospital-direct procurement under specific patient request, and NGO-mediated importation under humanitarian framework. Compassionate use, clinical research phase products, rare disease treatments, and superior therapeutic options qualify.
key_actors: INHRR Review Board (Junta Revisora) - issues special authorization. MPPS - issues import permit after INHRR authorization. SACS (Servicio Autonomo de Contraloria Sanitaria) - sanitary control oversight. SIVERC (Sistema Venezolano de Evaluacion y Registro de Productos Sanitarios) - online evaluation/registration platform launched March 15, 2023.
formulary_coverage: Special authorization mechanism applies specifically to drugs NOT on national formulary or NOT registered in Venezuela - bypasses formulary restrictions for individual patient or limited-quantity import.
judicial_access_volume: Not applicable directly; however INHRR authorizations sometimes triggered following amparo de salud judgments in favor of individual patients (mechanism not systematized). [uncertain: volume]
registration_timeline_generic: Standard INHRR registration of pharmaceutical specialties (Categoria A through F): official process ~6-12 months but documented to take 18-36+ months in practice. [uncertain: precise current 2024-2025 average]
registration_timeline_biologic: Longer than generic; full dossier biologics historically 24-48 months. [uncertain: exact 2024-2025 figure]
registration_authority: Instituto Nacional de Higiene Rafael Rangel (INHRR). Founded October 17, 1938. Attached to Ministerio del Poder Popular para la Salud (MPPS). Designated WHO National Reference Center for Registration and Control of Medicines (1996). Headquartered in Caracas. Phone: (+58 212) 219-1600 / 219-1622. Web: inhrr.gob.ve.
biosimilar_pathway: INHRR follows general biological products registration framework. No published distinct biosimilar guideline. [uncertain: specific comparability requirements and approved biosimilars]
registration_backlog: Documented but specific numbers not publicly disclosed. SIVERC online portal (March 2023) intended to address backlog. [uncertain: pending application count]
reform_status: enacted - SIVERC electronic registration system launched March 15, 2023; INHRR continues issuing periodic Avisos and Circulares. INHRR published updated 'Procedimiento de Registro Sanitario Instructivo General' (January 2026 revision available). Special-import authorization framework remains stable.
currency_mechanism: Registration fees historically in bolivars; importer payments in USD via private channels. INHRR fees subject to periodic adjustment via MPPS resolution.
pharma_import_cost_impact: Special-import authorization mechanism allows access to drugs unavailable through normal channels but at significant private cost; importers absorb compliance and currency-conversion costs. [uncertain: cost impact data]
financial_mechanism: out_of_pocket primarily for special-import authorized drugs (patient/family pays importer). Some NGO-funded imports under humanitarian framework. No reimbursement mechanism for non-registered drugs through IVSS or MPPS.
payer_debt_crisis: Not directly applicable to INHRR but importers face MPPS payment delays for any government procurement.
shortage_rate: Special-import mechanism is response to chronic shortage; effective utilization rate unclear. [uncertain: number of special authorizations granted annually 2020-2025]
shortage_causes: INHRR registration backlog itself contributes to drug unavailability - many international drugs lack Venezuelan sanitary registration, requiring special-import workaround. Causes: (1) reduced INHRR capacity due to staff exodus and resource constraints; (2) manufacturer withdrawal from Venezuelan market making registration uneconomic; (3) FX/payment difficulties limiting registration application volume.
shortage_monitoring_system: No formal INHRR shortage monitoring system. CENAVIF (Centro Nacional de Vigilancia Farmacologica) within INHRR handles pharmacovigilance, not shortage monitoring.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: Special-import authorization is documented as the route used for oncology rare-treatment access (e.g., novel targeted therapies); specific event log not public. [uncertain]
source_citation: 1) Lexology, 'In review: the life sciences regulatory regime in Venezuela', https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=b0398176-ddb9-4596-a8c9-9dfbfce0f89a. 2) INHRR official portal, https://inhrr.gob.ve. 3) INHRR, 'Procedimiento de Registro Sanitario Instructivo General' (Jan 2026), https://inhrr.gob.ve/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/. 4) INHRR, 'Instructivo para Elaborar las Solicitudes de Registro SIVERC 2023' (May 2023), https://inhrr.gob.ve/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/. 5) INHRR SIVERC launch announcement (March 15, 2023), https://inhrr.gob.ve/?p=7868.
data_reliability: medium - regulatory framework documented in official instructivos; actual operational data and timeline metrics opaque
kb_action: new_fact_add_to_doc - confirm 6-month INHRR Review Board special-import authorization is the formal exception mechanism; document SIVERC launch March 2023
